signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + USD Liquidity Demand
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5672 as the USD (G10 Leader) outperforms NZD (G10 Laggard) by 1.802% on a weekly basis. The move is accelerated by a 'flight to liquidity' as global equities slump (S&P 500 -1.44%, Nikkei -3.55%), driving USD to a 13-month high. Despite a 150bp carry advantage for USD, NZD COT positioning shows an extreme net short (long% 18.4%), creating a high-conviction trend that is now testing multi-year lows. The real rate differential (+1.48% USD vs -0.83% NZD) further reinforces the structural downside for the pair.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.51, holding above the critical 160.00 level that previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. A +264bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.62% vs TONA 0.98%) sustains the bid despite a moderate put skew in options (25d RR at -1.38) signaling institutional hedging for a drop. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 86% short, diverging from the institutional carry-driven long bias. Intervention risk is now acute on any further extension above 161.50.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning + CPI Risk
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6919 as IV (19.8%) trades at a massive +11.4% premium over realized HV30 (8.4%), pricing in extreme event risk ahead of the Australian CPI release. While the RBA maintains a 4.35% rate (60bp carry advantage over Fed), COT positioning is crowded long (70.2%), leaving the pair vulnerable to a long-unwind if inflation data misses. Negative CESI (-8) for AUD confirms a trend of data disappointments relative to USD (+30).

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4208 exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (z = -2.00σ), with the pair climbing despite oil settling below its 200-day MA ($73.21). The move is driven by broad USD strength and a +129bp OIS carry advantage rather than energy fundamentals. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 96% short, suggesting a crowded contrarian fade that may fuel further upside as shorts are squeezed. Watch for a break of the 1.4250 resistance level.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Complacency + Carry Pressure
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1388 remains under pressure from a +144bp OIS carry deficit (SOFR 3.62% vs €STR 2.18%). Options market shows a VRP of +2.1%, the lowest among G10 majors, suggesting relative complacency despite the pair's 2.00% decline over the last month. With the ECB hiking bias neutralized by a 'Hold' market bias, the 1.1350 level is the primary support to watch ahead of the July ECB meeting.
